orchestrator_corrections.reason: DR-026 surfaced via source-head class-of-bug expansion across executor-config/executor-audit/bayesian-scorer/prompt-pack samples

trend_note: absolute findings count narrowing (11/5/8/2) but new_info_ratio holds at 1.00; soft saturation signal emerging

description: Catalog+playbook describe Fallback router as 'Chooses whether a failed model should fall back to a configured target or fail fast' but source JSDoc says 'Resolve the fallback route when a model exhausts its quota pool' — docs emphasize generic configured-target switching while source emphasizes quota-pool exhaustion as the trigger condition. The 'fail fast' phrasing in docs maps to the source's two-route output (route or fail-fast) but the trigger condition is misrepresented.
recommended_patch: Update catalog F003 description + playbook DLR-003 §1 OVERVIEW to: 'Resolves the fallback route when a model exhausts its quota pool, returning either a fallback-target route or a fail-fast reason.'

description: Catalog+playbook describe Bayesian scorer as 'Scores executor reliability and decides when enough evidence supports demotion' — over-specializes a general primitive. The module is a 2-function primitive: computeScore (Laplace-smoothed probability, general-purpose) + shouldDemote (executor-specific demotion threshold). The module-level header and computeScore JSDoc are general; only shouldDemote is executor-specific. Docs conflate function-level specificity with module scope.
recommended_patch: Update catalog F010 description + playbook DLR-010 §1 OVERVIEW to: 'Provides Laplace-smoothed probability scoring (computeScore) plus an executor-demotion threshold check (shouldDemote) for runtime routing decisions.'
